data.content:
Here's my analysis of all legacy patterns found and the modernization plan:

**Legacy patterns identified:**

| # | Pattern | Action |
|---|---------|--------|
| 1 | Non-SDK `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` | → `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit MSBuild imports (Common.props, CSharp.targets) | Remove — SDK handles this |
| 3 | Default properties (Configuration, Platform, ProjectGuid, FileAlignment, Deterministic, AutoGenerateBindingRedirects) | Remove — all SDK defaults |
| 4 | Debug/Release PropertyGroups (DebugSymbols, DebugType, Optimize, OutputPath, DefineConstants, ErrorReport, WarningLevel) | Remove — all SDK defaults |
| 5 | Explicit framework references (System, System.Core, etc.) | Remove — implicit for .NET Framework |
| 6 | Explicit `<Compile Include="..."/>` items | Remove — SDK uses file globbing |
| 7 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Remove — move metadata to `<PropertyGroup>` |
| 8 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 9 | `RootNamespace` / `AssemblyName` matching project name | Remove — inferred from filename |

Now applying the changes:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize:

1. **Non-SDK-style project format** — Uses `ToolsVersion="15.0"` and `xmlns` declaration. SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`.

2. **Explicit MSBuild imports** — `Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)..."` and `Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets"` are handled automatically by the SDK.

3. **Default properties that are now implicit** — `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic` are all defaults in SDK-style.
   
The configuration-specific PropertyGroups for Debug and Release, explicit framework assembly references like `System` and `System.Core`, and the manual `Compile` includes are all unnecessary in SDK-style since they're handled through globbing and defaults. Assembly metadata from AssemblyInfo.cs can be moved directly into the project file, and the framework version should be updated from v4.7.2 to use the modern `TargetFramework` property.

Now I'm going to refactor the project file to remove these redundancies and apply SDK-style conventions.

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>

</Project>

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 12 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

**Removed (all handled by SDK defaults):**
- MSBuild imports & XML namespace
- Configuration/Platform/ProjectGuid/FileAlignment/Deterministic/AutoGenerateBindingRedirects
- Entire Debug & Release PropertyGroups
- All framework references (System, System.Core, etc.)
- All `<Compile>` items (SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs`)
- `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` (metadata moved to `<PropertyGroup>`)
- `RootNamespace`/`AssemblyName` (inferred from filename)

**Preserved as explicit properties:**
- `TargetFramework` (`net472`), `OutputType`, `Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`
